meta.title: Today's One Question | Mallo – Free Daily Check-in
meta.description: One gentle question to pause and notice how you are. No signup. Different question every day. From the team behind Mallo.
meta.intro: One question. No account. Just a moment to pause.
meta.ctaHeadline: Want this every day—and someone who remembers your answer?
meta.ctaBody: In the app, Mallo asks you one thing at a time, remembers what you shared, and checks in on you so you're not always the one starting the conversation. Same vibe—every day, and it grows with you.

questions.question: What's one small thing that went okay today—even if the rest felt messy?
questions.response: Noticing one thing counts. You don't have to fix everything to have done something right.

questions.question: Who's one person you're glad exists in your life right now?
questions.response: They don't have to know. Just naming them is enough.

questions.question: What did you need more of today—rest, movement, or someone to talk to?
questions.response: Knowing what you need is the first step. You're allowed to want it.

questions.question: If you could describe today in one word, what would it be?
questions.response: One word is enough. You don't owe anyone a full report.

questions.question: What's something you're looking forward to—even if it's small or far away?
questions.response: Looking forward is a kind of hope. It matters.

questions.question: What would make tomorrow feel a little easier?
questions.response: One thing. You don't have to solve the whole week—just one thing.

questions.question: When did you last take a real break—no screen, no task—even for five minutes?
questions.response: If it's been a while, that's information, not a verdict. Tomorrow is a new chance.

questions.question: What's one thing you'd want a friend to ask you about right now?
questions.response: Sometimes we just need someone to ask. You're allowed to want that.

questions.question: What are you proud of from this week—even if it feels small?
questions.response: Small counts. You showed up. That's worth naming.

questions.question: What's weighing on you that you haven't said out loud yet?
questions.response: You don't have to fix it right now. Naming it is already something.

questions.question: What made you smile or laugh recently—even briefly?
questions.response: Brief still counts. Joy doesn't have to last hours to be real.

questions.question: If your body could say one thing to you right now, what would it be?
questions.response: Listening to your body isn't selfish. It's how you show up for yourself.

questions.question: What's one boundary you want to keep this week?
questions.response: Boundaries aren't mean. They're how you stay enough for yourself.

questions.question: What do you wish someone had said to you today?
questions.response: Sometimes we have to say it to ourselves first. You're allowed to need that.

questions.question: What's one thing you're grateful for that has nothing to do with productivity?
questions.response: You don't have to earn rest or joy. Gratitude can be for the small, useless things too.

usageNote: Rotate by day-of-year or hash of (date + slug) so the same calendar day shows the same question for everyone. Example: index = (dayOfYear + 0) % questions.length for consistent daily rotation.
